purpose: Underlying data for reports/FY2025-26_sample_report.html/.pdf -- a consolidated, unit-standardized (US$ Million) FY2025-26 snapshot re-derived from this repo's other datasets, with a cross-source consistency-check log and period-matched PLI currency conversions.
currency_conversion_methodology: Each rupee-crore figure is converted using the AVERAGE of this repo's own FY-average USD/INR rates across the fiscal years the scheme has actually been active/disbursing -- not one blanket rate applied to every row. A scheme approved in FY2021-22 with disbursement through FY2024-25 is averaged over those four FY-average rates; a scheme launched in FY2025-26 (no completed FY average exists yet) uses the latest completed FY average (FY2024-25) as the closest available proxy, flagged distinctly. This corrects an earlier version of this report that applied a single FY2024-25 rate to every scheme regardless of its own timeline.

pli_scheme_funding_usd_million.period_averaged: FY2024-25 only (launched Nov 2025 / FY2025-26, no completed FY avg yet)

consistency_checks.text: Import totals agree across TRADESTAT ($776,013.7M) and TIA Portal ($776,013.65M) to within $0.05M.
HS27 export chapter matches exactly between TRADESTAT ($55,871.2M) and TIA Portal ($55,871.21M).
PIB's official export/deficit figures ($441,780.0M / -$333,190.0M) agree with TRADESTAT within 0.3%.
TIA Portal's export total ($451,070.0M) reads 2.1% / $9,300M higher than TRADESTAT and PIB -- flagged as a data-vintage difference, not reconciled further.

Two forex-reserves vintages exist: the MoSPI-connector series (stale at $698,100.0M, Jun 2025) and this repo's own RBI weekly scrape ($675,157.0M, 10 Jul 2026) -- kept visually and numerically distinct, never silently merged.
No FY2025-26 services-trade figure exists yet -- RBI's services data runs one year behind goods trade.
All PLI rupee crore figures now converted at each scheme's own elapsed-period average rate (Rs 77.91-84.68/US$ depending on scheme), not one blanket rate.
Top-12 chapter and country-ranking figures cross-checked against this repo's own hsn_historical_trends, sector_country_priority, and export_destination_priority datasets.

NITI Aayog's Trade Watch Quarterly (PIB PRID=2277232, released 23 Jun 2026) reports India's FY2025-26 total trade (goods+services) at $1.84 trillion with exports +4.2% / imports +6.5% y-o-y (blended). This repo's own goods-only TRADESTAT figures show export growth of only +0.92% and import growth of +7.60% FY2024-25 to FY2025-26 -- the gap is explained by services trade growing faster than goods on the export side (NITI Aayog cites +9.0% services export growth for the same period), not a data error.

NITI Aayog's Trade Watch Quarterly Q4 FY2025-26 thematic focus (pharmaceuticals) cites India's pharmaceutical + API exports at $35.8bn against global API/pharma demand of ~$1.3 trillion. This repo's own HS30 (pharmaceutical formulations only) FY2025-26 export figure is $25,073.4M -- the ~$10.7bn gap is API exports sitting in HS29 (organic chemicals), consistent with this repo's own PLI Bulk Drugs finding that Bulk Drugs PLI supports HS30 indirectly via HS29.

external_validation_sources.source: NITI Aayog Trade Watch Quarterly, 8th edition, Q4 FY2025-26 (Jan-Mar 2026)
external_validation_sources.via: PIB press release PRID=2277232, released 23 Jun 2026

external_validation_sources.source: USISPF U.S.-India Bilateral Trade Data Analysis 2022
external_validation_sources.note: Calendar-year (not fiscal-year) US BEA data -- confirms India ran a goods+services trade SURPLUS with the USA of $45.68bn in CY2022, consistent with this repo's own finding that the USA is India's one major partner in overall bilateral surplus.

external_validation_sources.source: IJCRT2105171, "An Analytical Study On Indian Import Export System Of India" (2021, using FY2016-17 data)
external_validation_sources.note: Confirms China was already India's largest import-source AND largest trade-deficit country back in FY2016-17, and the USA was already the largest trade-surplus country -- both findings are decade-plus structural features, not recent developments.
